1738	def _min_or_max_filter(input, size, footprint, structure, output, mode,
1739	                       cval, origin, minimum, axes=None):
1740	    if (size is not None) and (footprint is not None):
1741	        warnings.warn("ignoring size because footprint is set",
1742	                      UserWarning, stacklevel=3)
1743	    if structure is None:
1744	        if footprint is None:
1745	            if size is None:
1746	                raise RuntimeError("no footprint provided")
1747	            separable = True
1748	        else:
1749	            footprint = np.asarray(footprint, dtype=bool)
1750	            if not footprint.any():
1751	                raise ValueError("All-zero footprint is not supported.")
1752	            if footprint.all():
1753	                size = footprint.shape
1754	                footprint = None
1755	                separable = True
1756	            else:
1757	                separable = False
1758	    else:
1759	        structure = np.asarray(structure, dtype=np.float64)
1760	        separable = False
1761	        if footprint is None:
1762	            footprint = np.ones(structure.shape, bool)
1763	        else:
1764	            footprint = np.asarray(footprint, dtype=bool)
1765	    input = np.asarray(input)
1766	    if np.iscomplexobj(input):
1767	        raise TypeError("Complex type not supported")
1768	    output = _ni_support._get_output(output, input)
1769	    temp_needed = np.may_share_memory(input, output)
1770	    if temp_needed:
1771	        # input and output arrays cannot share memory
1772	        temp = output
1773	        output = _ni_support._get_output(output.dtype, input)
1774	    axes = _ni_support._check_axes(axes, input.ndim)
1775	    num_axes = len(axes)
1776	    if separable:
1777	        origins = _ni_support._normalize_sequence(origin, num_axes)
1778	        sizes = _ni_support._normalize_sequence(size, num_axes)
1779	        modes = _ni_support._normalize_sequence(mode, num_axes)
1780	        axes = [(axes[ii], sizes[ii], origins[ii], modes[ii])
1781	                for ii in range(len(axes)) if sizes[ii] > 1]
1782	        if minimum:
1783	            filter_ = minimum_filter1d
1784	        else:
1785	            filter_ = maximum_filter1d
1786	        if len(axes) > 0:
1787	            for axis, size, origin, mode in axes:
1788	                filter_(input, int(size), axis, output, mode, cval, origin)
1789	                input = output
1790	        else:
1791	            output[...] = input[...]
1792	    else:
1793	        # expand origins and footprint if num_axes < input.ndim
1794	        footprint = _expand_footprint(input.ndim, axes, footprint)
1795	        origins = _expand_origin(input.ndim, axes, origin)
1796	
1797	        fshape = [ii for ii in footprint.shape if ii > 0]
1798	        if len(fshape) != input.ndim:
1799	            raise RuntimeError(f"footprint.ndim ({footprint.ndim}) must match "
1800	                               f"len(axes) ({len(axes)})")
1801	        for origin, lenf in zip(origins, fshape):
1802	            if (lenf // 2 + origin < 0) or (lenf // 2 + origin >= lenf):
1803	                raise ValueError("invalid origin")
1804	        if not footprint.flags.contiguous:
1805	            footprint = footprint.copy()
1806	        if structure is not None:
1807	            if len(structure.shape) != num_axes:
1808	                raise RuntimeError("structure array has incorrect shape")
1809	            if num_axes != structure.ndim:
1810	                structure = np.expand_dims(
1811	                    structure,
1812	                    tuple(ax for ax in range(structure.ndim) if ax not in axes)
1813	                )
1814	            if not structure.flags.contiguous:
1815	                structure = structure.copy()
1816	        if not isinstance(mode, str) and isinstance(mode, Iterable):
1817	            raise RuntimeError(
1818	                "A sequence of modes is not supported for non-separable "
1819	                "footprints")
1820	        mode = _ni_support._extend_mode_to_code(mode)
1821	        _nd_image.min_or_max_filter(input, footprint, structure, output,
1822	                                    mode, cval, origins, minimum)
1823	    if temp_needed:
1824	        temp[...] = output
1825	        output = temp
1826	    return output
1827

1830	def minimum_filter(input, size=None, footprint=None, output=None,
1831	                   mode="reflect", cval=0.0, origin=0, *, axes=None):
1832	    """Calculate a multidimensional minimum filter.
1833	
1834	    Parameters
1835	    ----------

1837	    %(size_foot)s
1838	    %(output)s

1844	        `input` is filtered along the specified axes. When `axes` is
1845	        specified, any tuples used for `size`, `origin`, and/or `mode`
1846	        must match the length of `axes`. The ith entry in any of these tuples
1847	        corresponds to the ith entry in `axes`.

1849	    Returns

1852	        Filtered array. Has the same shape as `input`.
1853	
1854	    Notes

1858	
1859	    Examples
1860	    --------

1928	def _rank_filter(input, rank, size=None, footprint=None, output=None,
1929	                 mode="reflect", cval=0.0, origin=0, operation='rank',
1930	                 axes=None):
1931	    if (size is not None) and (footprint is not None):
1932	        warnings.warn("ignoring size because footprint is set",
1933	                      UserWarning, stacklevel=3)
1934	    input = np.asarray(input)
1935	    if np.iscomplexobj(input):
1936	        raise TypeError('Complex type not supported')
1937	    axes = _ni_support._check_axes(axes, input.ndim)
1938	    num_axes = len(axes)
1939	    if footprint is None:
1940	        if size is None:
1941	            raise RuntimeError("no footprint or filter size provided")
1942	        sizes = _ni_support._normalize_sequence(size, num_axes)
1943	        footprint = np.ones(sizes, dtype=bool)
1944	    else:
1945	        footprint = np.asarray(footprint, dtype=bool)
1946	    # expand origins, footprint and modes if num_axes < input.ndim
1947	    footprint = _expand_footprint(input.ndim, axes, footprint)
1948	    origins = _expand_origin(input.ndim, axes, origin)
1949	    mode = _expand_mode(input.ndim, axes, mode)
1950	
1951	    fshape = [ii for ii in footprint.shape if ii > 0]
1952	    if len(fshape) != input.ndim:
1953	        raise RuntimeError(f"footprint.ndim ({footprint.ndim}) must match "
1954	                           f"len(axes) ({len(axes)})")
1955	    for origin, lenf in zip(origins, fshape):
1956	        if (lenf // 2 + origin < 0) or (lenf // 2 + origin >= lenf):
1957	            raise ValueError('invalid origin')
1958	    if not footprint.flags.contiguous:
1959	        footprint = footprint.copy()
1960	    filter_size = np.where(footprint, 1, 0).sum()
1961	    if operation == 'median':
1962	        rank = filter_size // 2
1963	    elif operation == 'percentile':
1964	        percentile = rank
1965	        if percentile < 0.0:
1966	            percentile += 100.0
1967	        if percentile < 0 or percentile > 100:
1968	            raise RuntimeError('invalid percentile')
1969	        if percentile == 100.0:
1970	            rank = filter_size - 1
1971	        else:
1972	            rank = int(float(filter_size) * percentile / 100.0)
1973	    if rank < 0:
1974	        rank += filter_size
1975	    if rank < 0 or rank >= filter_size:
1976	        raise RuntimeError('rank not within filter footprint size')
1977	    if rank == 0:
1978	        return minimum_filter(input, None, footprint, output, mode, cval,
1979	                              origins, axes=None)
1980	    elif rank == filter_size - 1:
1981	        return maximum_filter(input, None, footprint, output, mode, cval,
1982	                              origins, axes=None)
1983	    else:
1984	        output = _ni_support._get_output(output, input)
1985	        temp_needed = np.may_share_memory(input, output)
1986	        if temp_needed:
1987	            # input and output arrays cannot share memory
1988	            temp = output
1989	            output = _ni_support._get_output(output.dtype, input)
1990	        if not isinstance(mode, str) and isinstance(mode, Iterable):
1991	            raise RuntimeError(
1992	                "A sequence of modes is not supported by non-separable rank "
1993	                "filters")
1994	        mode = _ni_support._extend_mode_to_code(mode, is_filter=True)
1995	        # Some corner cases are currently not allowed to use the
1996	        # "new"/fast 1D rank filter code, including when the
1997	        # footprint is large compared to the array size.
1998	        # See discussion in gh-23293; longer-term it may be possible
1999	        # to allow the fast path for these corner cases as well,
2000	        # if algorithmic fixes are found.
2001	        lim2 = input.size - ((footprint.size - 1) // 2 - origin)
2002	        if input.ndim == 1 and ((lim2 >= 0) or (input.size == 1)):
2003	            if input.dtype in (np.int64, np.float64, np.float32):
2004	                x = input
2005	                x_out = output
2006	            elif input.dtype == np.float16:
2007	                x = input.astype('float32')
2008	                x_out = np.empty(x.shape, dtype='float32')
2009	            elif np.result_type(input, np.int64) == np.int64:
2010	                x = input.astype('int64')
2011	                x_out = np.empty(x.shape, dtype='int64')
2012	            elif input.dtype.kind in 'biu':
2013	                # cast any other boolean, integer or unsigned type to int64
2014	                x = input.astype('int64')
2015	                x_out = np.empty(x.shape, dtype='int64')

2020	                                        origin)

2022	                np.copyto(output, x_out, casting='unsafe')

2024	            _nd_image.rank_filter(input, rank, footprint, output, mode, cval, origins)
2025	        if temp_needed:
2026	            temp[...] = output
2027	            output = temp
2028	        return output

2189	def generic_filter1d(input, function, filter_size, axis=-1,
2190	                     output=None, mode="reflect", cval=0.0, origin=0,
2191	                     extra_arguments=(), extra_keywords=None):
2192	    """Calculate a 1-D filter along the given axis.
2193	
2194	    `generic_filter1d` iterates over the lines of the array, calling the
2195	    given function at each line. The arguments of the line are the
2196	    input line, and the output line. The input and output lines are 1-D
2197	    double arrays. The input line is extended appropriately according
2198	    to the filter size and origin. The output line must be modified
2199	    in-place with the result.
2200	
2201	    Parameters
2202	    ----------
2203	    %(input)s
2204	    function : {callable, scipy.LowLevelCallable}
2205	        Function to apply along given axis.
2206	    filter_size : scalar
2207	        Length of the filter.
2208	    %(axis)s
2209	    %(output)s
2210	    %(mode_reflect)s
2211	    %(cval)s
2212	    %(origin)s
2213	    %(extra_arguments)s
2214	    %(extra_keywords)s
2215	
2216	    Returns
2217	    -------
2218	    generic_filter1d : ndarray
2219	        Filtered array. Has the same shape as `input`.
2220	
2221	    Notes
2222	    -----
2223	    This function also accepts low-level callback functions with one of
2224	    the following signatures and wrapped in `scipy.LowLevelCallable`:
2225	
2226	    .. code:: c
2227	
2228	       int function(double *input_line, npy_intp input_length,
2229	                    double *output_line, npy_intp output_length,
2230	                    void *user_data)
2231	       int function(double *input_line, intptr_t input_length,
2232	                    double *output_line, intptr_t output_length,
2233	                    void *user_data)
2234	
2235	    The calling function iterates over the lines of the input and output
2236	    arrays, calling the callback function at each line. The current line
2237	    is extended according to the border conditions set by the calling
2238	    function, and the result is copied into the array that is passed
2239	    through ``input_line``. The length of the input line (after extension)
2240	    is passed through ``input_length``. The callback function should apply
2241	    the filter and store the result in the array passed through
2242	    ``output_line``. The length of the output line is passed through
2243	    ``output_length``. ``user_data`` is the data pointer provided
2244	    to `scipy.LowLevelCallable` as-is.
2245	
2246	    The callback function must return an integer error status that is zero
2247	    if something went wrong and one otherwise. If an error occurs, you should
2248	    normally set the python error status with an informative message
2249	    before returning, otherwise a default error message is set by the
2250	    calling function.
2251	
2252	    In addition, some other low-level function pointer specifications
2253	    are accepted, but these are for backward compatibility only and should
2254	    not be used in new code.
2255	
2256	    Examples
2257	    --------
2258	    This example defines and applies a custom callback function that computes
2259	    the range (maximum minus minimum) within a sliding window of size 3.
2260	    It utilizes `numpy.lib.stride_tricks.sliding_window_view` and demonstrates the
2261	    required in-place modification of the ``output_line`` array.
2262	
2263	    >>> import numpy as np
2264	    >>> from scipy.ndimage import generic_filter1d
2265	    >>> from numpy.lib.stride_tricks import sliding_window_view
2266	
2267	    >>> def local_range(input_line, output_line):
2268	    ...     # input_line includes padded values according to `filter_size` and `mode`
2269	    ...     v = sliding_window_view(input_line, 3)
2270	    ...     # modify `output_line` in-place rather than returning the result
2271	    ...     output_line[:] = v.max(axis=1) - v.min(axis=1)
2272	
2273	    >>> x = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], dtype=np.float64)
2274	    >>> generic_filter1d(x, local_range, filter_size=3)
2275	    array([1., 2., 2., 2., 1.])
2276	
2277	    """
2278	    if extra_keywords is None:
2279	        extra_keywords = {}
2280	    input = np.asarray(input)
2281	    if np.iscomplexobj(input):
2282	        raise TypeError('Complex type not supported')
2283	    output = _ni_support._get_output(output, input)
2284	    if filter_size < 1:
2285	        raise RuntimeError('invalid filter size')
2286	    axis = normalize_axis_index(axis, input.ndim)
2287	    if (filter_size // 2 + origin < 0) or (filter_size // 2 + origin >=
2288	                                           filter_size):
2289	        raise ValueError('invalid origin')

2291	    _nd_image.generic_filter1d(input, function, filter_size, axis, output,
2292	                               mode, cval, origin, extra_arguments,
2293	                               extra_keywords)
2294	    return output

2298	def generic_filter(input, function, size=None, footprint=None,
2299	                   output=None, mode="reflect", cval=0.0, origin=0,
2300	                   extra_arguments=(), extra_keywords=None, *, axes=None):
2301	    """Calculate a multidimensional filter using the given function.
2302	
2303	    At each element the provided function is called. The input values
2304	    within the filter footprint at that element are passed to the function
2305	    as a 1-D array of double values.
2306	
2307	    Parameters
2308	    ----------
2309	    %(input)s
2310	    function : {callable, scipy.LowLevelCallable}
2311	        Function to apply at each element.
2312	    %(size_foot)s
2313	    %(output)s
2314	    %(mode_reflect)s
2315	    %(cval)s
2316	    %(origin_multiple)s
2317	    %(extra_arguments)s
2318	    %(extra_keywords)s
2319	    axes : tuple of int or None, optional
2320	        If None, `input` is filtered along all axes. Otherwise,
2321	        `input` is filtered along the specified axes. When `axes` is
2322	        specified, any tuples used for `size` or `origin` must match the length
2323	        of `axes`. The ith entry in any of these tuples corresponds to the ith
2324	        entry in `axes`.
2325	
2326	    Returns
2327	    -------
2328	    output : ndarray
2329	        Filtered array. Has the same shape as `input`.
2330	
2331	    See Also
2332	    --------
2333	    vectorized_filter : similar functionality, but optimized for vectorized callables
2334	
2335	    Notes
2336	    -----
2337	    This function is ideal for use with instances of `scipy.LowLevelCallable`;
2338	    for vectorized, pure-Python callables, consider `vectorized_filter` for improved
2339	    performance.
2340	
2341	    Low-level callback functions must have one of the following signatures:
2342	
2343	    .. code:: c
2344	
2345	       int callback(double *buffer, npy_intp filter_size,
2346	                    double *return_value, void *user_data)
2347	       int callback(double *buffer, intptr_t filter_size,
2348	                    double *return_value, void *user_data)
2349	
2350	    The calling function iterates over the elements of the input and
2351	    output arrays, calling the callback function at each element. The
2352	    elements within the footprint of the filter at the current element are
2353	    passed through the ``buffer`` parameter, and the number of elements
2354	    within the footprint through ``filter_size``. The calculated value is
2355	    returned in ``return_value``. ``user_data`` is the data pointer provided
2356	    to `scipy.LowLevelCallable` as-is.
2357	
2358	    The callback function must return an integer error status that is zero
2359	    if something went wrong and one otherwise. If an error occurs, you should
2360	    normally set the python error status with an informative message
2361	    before returning, otherwise a default error message is set by the
2362	    calling function.
2363	
2364	    In addition, some other low-level function pointer specifications
2365	    are accepted, but these are for backward compatibility only and should
2366	    not be used in new code.
2367	
2368	    Examples
2369	    --------
2370	    Import the necessary modules and load the example image used for
2371	    filtering.
2372	
2373	    >>> import numpy as np
2374	    >>> from scipy import datasets
2375	    >>> from scipy.ndimage import zoom, generic_filter
2376	    >>> import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
2377	    >>> ascent = zoom(datasets.ascent(), 0.5)
2378	
2379	    Compute a maximum filter with kernel size 5 by passing a simple NumPy
2380	    aggregation function as argument to `function`.
2381	
2382	    >>> maximum_filter_result = generic_filter(ascent, np.amax, [5, 5])
2383	
2384	    While a maximum filter could also directly be obtained using
2385	    `maximum_filter`, `generic_filter` allows generic Python function or
2386	    `scipy.LowLevelCallable` to be used as a filter. Here, we compute the
2387	    range between maximum and minimum value as an example for a kernel size
2388	    of 5.
2389	
2390	    >>> def custom_filter(image):
2391	    ...     return np.amax(image) - np.amin(image)
2392	    >>> custom_filter_result = generic_filter(ascent, custom_filter, [5, 5])
2393	
2394	    Plot the original and filtered images.
2395	
2396	    >>> fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(3, 9))
2397	    >>> plt.gray()  # show the filtered result in grayscale
2398	    >>> top, middle, bottom = axes
2399	    >>> for ax in axes:
2400	    ...     ax.set_axis_off()  # remove coordinate system
2401	    >>> top.imshow(ascent)
2402	    >>> top.set_title("Original image")
2403	    >>> middle.imshow(maximum_filter_result)
2404	    >>> middle.set_title("Maximum filter, Kernel: 5x5")
2405	    >>> bottom.imshow(custom_filter_result)
2406	    >>> bottom.set_title("Custom filter, Kernel: 5x5")
2407	    >>> fig.tight_layout()
2408	
2409	    """
2410	    if (size is not None) and (footprint is not None):
2411	        warnings.warn("ignoring size because footprint is set",
2412	                      UserWarning, stacklevel=2)
2413	    if extra_keywords is None:
2414	        extra_keywords = {}
2415	    input = np.asarray(input)
2416	    if np.iscomplexobj(input):
2417	        raise TypeError('Complex type not supported')
2418	    axes = _ni_support._check_axes(axes, input.ndim)
2419	    num_axes = len(axes)
2420	    if footprint is None:
2421	        if size is None:
2422	            raise RuntimeError("no footprint or filter size provided")
2423	        sizes = _ni_support._normalize_sequence(size, num_axes)
2424	        footprint = np.ones(sizes, dtype=bool)
2425	    else:
2426	        footprint = np.asarray(footprint, dtype=bool)
2427	
2428	    # expand origins, footprint if num_axes < input.ndim
2429	    footprint = _expand_footprint(input.ndim, axes, footprint)
2430	    origins = _expand_origin(input.ndim, axes, origin)
2431	
2432	    fshape = [ii for ii in footprint.shape if ii > 0]
2433	    if len(fshape) != input.ndim:
2434	        raise RuntimeError(f"footprint.ndim ({footprint.ndim}) "
2435	                           f"must match len(axes) ({num_axes})")
2436	    for origin, lenf in zip(origins, fshape):
2437	        if (lenf // 2 + origin < 0) or (lenf // 2 + origin >= lenf):
2438	            raise ValueError('invalid origin')
2439	    if not footprint.flags.contiguous:
2440	        footprint = footprint.copy()
2441	    output = _ni_support._get_output(output, input)
2442	
2443	    mode = _ni_support._extend_mode_to_code(mode)
2444	    _nd_image.generic_filter(input, function, footprint, output, mode,
2445	                             cval, origins, extra_arguments, extra_keywords)
2446	    return output
2447
